old_value: {"id":"SPARK2-9gm","title":"Implement GPU Instancing for splat rendering","description":"Replace per-point meshes with THREE.InstancedMesh to reduce draw calls from thousands to single digits.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-02T09:18:20Z","created_by":"alyoshakidoguchi","updated_at":"2026-03-02T09:18:20Z"}

new_value: Already implemented - MAX_PROXY_TRIANGLES_FOR_BVH = 1,500,000 at initScene.ts:216 with skip logic at lines 834-845

old_value: {"id":"SPARK2-t6d","title":"Remove console.log from hot paths","description":"Audit and remove/guard debug prints in render loop to prevent stalls when developer console is open.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T09:18:48Z","created_by":"alyoshakidoguchi","updated_at":"2026-03-02T09:18:48Z"}

new_value: No console statements in render loop (hot path). Console calls are only in initialization, error handlers, and one-time proxy loading events.
Added WebGPU detection in initViewer. renderBackend capability is now exposed via viewer.renderBackend. Detection runs asynchronously and logs which backend is available.
Implemented BufferPool class with size-based buckets. Reuses Uint8Array buffers across file loads to reduce GC pressure. Buckets: <=10MB, <=50MB, <=100MB, <=500MB, >500MB.

Implemented Web Worker for splat decoding. New option 'useWorker: true' in LoadOptions enables off-main-thread decoding. Worker is created as separate bundle (7.7MB). Currently disabled by default for stability.

Render loop already optimized: (1) Delta time clamped to 0.05s max, (2) orbitControls.update() only called when enabled, (3) No per-frame allocations in tick loop, (4) Event-driven UI updates (not per-frame).
Requires changes to external @sparkjsdev/spark library - not implementable in viewer. The SplatMesh rendering is handled entirely by the Spark module.

Implemented dynamic resolution scaling with FPS monitoring. Uses scale factors [1.0, 0.875, 0.75, 0.625, 0.5] when FPS drops below 30. Enabled via viewer.setDynamicResolution(true).
